content:
# Ic

**Summary**: An analytical technique used to separate and quantify ions/ions-like molecules in a liquid sample.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2009-zubair|Zubair, 2009]] used IC for the analysis of bromate ions in treated water samples.

- [[2023-al-aqeel|Al-Aqeel, 2023]] used Metrohm 850 Professional IC to measure the concentration of anions and cations in spent caustic samples.

- [[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025]] used IC to quantify calcium and magnesium concentrations.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2023-al-aqeel|Al-Aqeel, 2023]] used Metrohm 850 Professional IC to measure the concentration of anions and cations in spent caustic samples.

- [[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025]] used IC to quantify calcium and magnesium concentrations.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Used specifically for bromate analysis to ensure concentrations were below regulatory limits. ([[2009-zubair|Zubair, 2009, p. 28]])

- Used to determine concentrations of Na, K, Mg, Ca, F, Cl, NO2, Br, NO3, and SO4. ([[2023-al-aqeel|Al-Aqeel, 2023, p. 43]])

- Quantified Ca and Mg levels across various experimental conditions. ([[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025, p. 58]])
